sentence_reviews.text: Safety and policy alignment shape behavior with written principles, preference data, constraints, classifiers, refusal policies, and escalation rules.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports policies, controls, monitoring, and governance; RLHF/DPO papers support preference-data-based behavior shaping.
sentence_reviews.notes: Well supported as synthesis, but not an exact claim from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment is to connect Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization to downstream capabilities including Conversational assistants & chatbots, Browser, desktop & computer-use agents, Autonomous vehicles, drones & navigation.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RLHF/DPO support preference optimization; NIST supports safety governance; PML supports constrained/multi-objective optimization foundations.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible graph synthesis, though downstream examples are not directly sourced.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RLHF and DPO are presented for aligning language-model behavior; NIST supports safety governance for AI systems.
sentence_reviews.notes: Strongly plausible for assistants, but still framed as atlas substrate language.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Browser, desktop & computer-use agents
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST AI RMF supports safety controls and governance, but this packet lacks a specific computer-use-agent source.
sentence_reviews.notes: Plausible, but not directly established by the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Autonomous vehicles, drones & navigation
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST supports safety risk management; PML covers optimization foundations, but autonomous vehicle policy alignment is not specifically sourced.
sentence_reviews.notes: The claim is plausible but too broad for the available citations.

sentence_reviews.text: Coverage is necessarily incomplete: measured proxies, policies, and test suites cannot enumerate every deployment context or adversary. Its direct dependencies include Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families), Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: NIST emphasizes ongoing contextual risk management; RLHF/DPO/PML support the named dependency families.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is plausible but broad; dependency list is graph-derived.

sentence_reviews.text: Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families).
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RLHF/DPO directly support preference optimization for behavior alignment; NIST supports governance and safety controls.

sentence_reviews.text: Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Multi-objective, constrained & Pareto optimization.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML supports constrained and multi-objective optimization foundations; NIST supports balancing multiple risk-management objectives and constraints.
sentence_reviews.notes: Core relationship is defensible as synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Safety policies, constitutional constraints & alignment measures, constrains, audits, or governs behavior using Human evaluation & preference studies.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: RLHF uses human feedback for instruction following; NIST supports human-centered evaluation and governance.

edge_reviews.notes: Preference optimization is a core alignment mechanism for behavior shaping.
Safety policy alignment commonly involves constraints and tradeoffs; core strength is defensible.
Human evaluation/preference studies are a common input to policy alignment.
